reasoning_content:

Let's confirm using known calendar: 2026 is not a leap year (2024 is leap, so 2025 is not leap, 2026 not). Jan 1, 2026: Let's compute: 2025 is not a leap year, day offset from 2020: 2020 was a leap year. Let's compute day-of-week using Zeller's Congruence or known reference: We know Jan 1, 2020 was a Wednesday (2020 started on Wednesday). Let's count days to Jan 1, 2026. Number of days from Jan 1, 2020 to Jan 1, 2026: 2020 (leap) has 366 days, 2021 has 365, 2022 365, 2023 365, 2024 (leap) 366, 2025 365. Sum: 366+365+365+365+366+365 = 2192 days. 2192 mod 7 = 2192 - (7*313=2191) = 1. So Jan 1, 2026 is one day after Wednesday = Thursday? Wait: Starting from Wed (Jan 1 2020) + 1 day = Thursday. Actually we need to check: Jan 1, 2020 was a Wednesday. So Jan 1, 2021 is Thursday? Actually 2020 has 366 days, which is 52 weeks + 2 days. So Jan 1, 2021 is Wednesday + 2 days = Friday. Let's compute carefully: Jan 1, 2020: Wednesday. Jan 1, 2021 = Jan 1, 2020 + 366 days = +2 days mod 7 = Friday. Jan 1, 2022 = Jan 1, 2021 + 365 days = +1 day mod 7 = Saturday. Jan 1, 2023 = Jan 1, 2022 + 365 = +1 day = Sunday. Jan 1, 2024 = Jan 1, 2023 + 365 = +1 day = Monday. Jan 1, 2025 = Jan 1, 2024 + 366 (leap) = +2 days = Wednesday. Jan 1, 2026 = Jan 1, 2025 + 365 = +1 day = Thursday. So Jan 1, 2026 is Thursday. Jan 27, 2026 = Jan 1 + 26 days. 26 mod 7 = 5 days shift. So Thursday + 5 days = Tuesday? Let's see: Thursday + 1 = Friday (1), Saturday (2), Sunday (3), Monday (4), Tuesday (5). So Jan 27, 2026 is Tuesday. So day-of-week: Tuesday. So answer could also mention day-of-week.
